LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARSANNE WEESE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16) IS GRANTED AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO ALL THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM DEFENDANT'S PROTECTED CONDUCT OF COMMUNICATING WITH OTHER EMPLOYEES REGARDING THE EMPLOYMENT LAWSUIT AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND SENDING A PRE-LITIGATION DEMAND LETTER TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THE REQUISITE MINIMAL MERITS OF EITHER OF THESE CLAIMS. THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT ALLEGED AGAINST MS. WEESE. THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITIES. DEFENDANT HAS TEN DAYS TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Motion For Atty'S Fees
Off Calendar Mar-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Julia M. Adams, Esq., Law Offices, 415-658-5848, for defendant and moving party
Robert Tayac, Esq., Law Office, 415-552-6000, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified:
DEFENDANT MARSANNE WEESE Ntc Of Motion For Attyorney's Fees is Granted in part. Defendant is awarded fees and costs of $3,000, calculated as fees for 20 hours at $250 per hour and costs of $60. Because Defendant was only successful in striking two out of thirteen causes of action, although she is the prevailing party in the anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike, she is awarded a portion of what she seeks. The results of the motion were sufficiently significant such that defendant did achieve a practical benefit from bringing the motion. The percentage is commensurate with the extent to which the motion was successful and changed the nature and character of the lawsuit in a practical way. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Ntc Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jan-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARSANNE WEESE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16).
Appearances: Julia M. Adams of the Law Office of Julia M. Adams (658.5848) for defendant and moving party; David Wilton (669.4059) for plaintiff and responding party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted as to the sixth and seventh causes of action and denied as to all the remaining causes of action. The sixth and seventh causes of action arise from defendant's protected conduct of communicating with other employees regarding the employment lawsuit against plaintiff and sending a pre-litigation demand letter to plaintiff. Plaintiff has not shown the requisite minimal merits of either of these claims. The twelfth cause of action is not alleged against Ms. Weese. The remaining causes of action do not arise from protected activities. Defendant has ten days to file a responsive pleading. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
